name: La Cebadilla Trail
name_original: Las Cebadillas y Central Hidraúlica de Poqueira – trail running circular desde Capileira
description: Jog the difficult 5.3-mile La Cebadilla Trail in Sierra Nevada National Park, featuring mountain views and a historic ghost village.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Capileira offer a diverse experience within the Barranco de Poqueira gorge, nestled on the southern slopes of the Sierra Nevada. This region is characterized by dramatic mountain landscapes, including views of Mulhacén and Veleta, and varied terrain ranging from leafy paths to rocky, high-altitude trails. Runners can explore a network of routes that wind through traditional white villages and past impressive cliffs, providing a challenging yet rewarding environment. Best jogging routes around Capileira The most popular jogging route is La Cebadilla Trail, a 5.3 miles (8.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 11 minutes to complete, leading to the historic ghost village of La Cebadilla with mountain views. Another top favourite among local runners is Las Cebadillas Chapel loop from Capileira, a difficult 10.7 miles (17.2 km) path. This route offers extensive climbs and panoramic views across the high Sierra Nevada. Local runners also love the Bubión Village – Capileira Village loop from Capileira, a 3.7 miles (6.0 km) trail leading through the charming white villages of the Poqueira Gorge, often completed in about 42 minutes. Jogging around Capileira is defined by the dramatic Barranco de Poqueira gorge, the majestic Sierra Nevada mountains, and picturesque white villages. The network of routes offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Capileira are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 700 runners have used komoot to explore Capileira's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Capileira?
A: Capileira offers a wide variety of options for runners, with over 50 dedicated routes available. These range from easy strolls to challenging mountain runs, ensuring there's something for every fitness level.

Q: What makes jogging in Capileira a unique experience?
A: Jogging in Capileira is unique due to its stunning location within the &lt;strong&gt;Barranco de Poqueira gorge&lt;/strong&gt;, nestled on the southern slopes of the Sierra Nevada. Runners can enjoy dramatic mountain landscapes, including views of Mulhacén and Veleta, varied terrain from leafy paths to high-altitude trails, and the charm of traditional white villages. On clear days, you might even catch views across the Mediterranean Sea to Africa.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Capileira?
A: Yes, while many routes are challenging, there are a few easier options. The region offers at least 2 routes classified as easy, and 9 as moderate, perfect for those looking for a less strenuous run or just starting out. These often wind through orchards and open countryside, providing gentle inclines and beautiful scenery.

Q: Can I find long-distance running trails near Capileira?
A: Absolutely. Capileira is an excellent base for long-distance runners. The area is traversed by extensive paths like the GR-240 (Sulayr) and the GR-7 E-4 (European trail Tarifa-Peloponnese), offering numerous options for extended runs. For a challenging long-distance experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17237684" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Veleta Peak – View of Trevélez loop from PR-A 26 Hoya del Portillo - Refugio del Poqueira&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 41 km with significant elevation gain.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the Capileira area?
A: Yes, many of the running routes around Capileira are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A popular moderate circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33887597" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bubión Village – Capileira Village loop from Capileira&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the charming white villages of the Poqueira Gorge. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14598391" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Cebadillas Chapel loop from Capileira&lt;/a&gt;, a more difficult 17.2 km path offering panoramic views.

Q: What kind of views and landmarks can I expect along the running trails?
A: The running trails around Capileira offer spectacular views of the Sierra Nevada mountains, including the peaks of Mulhacén and Veleta. You'll also encounter the dramatic &lt;strong&gt;Barranco de Poqueira gorge&lt;/strong&gt; and picturesque white villages like Bubión and Pampaneira. Notable viewpoints include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/154326" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Veleta viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;strong&gt;Junta de los Rios&lt;/strong&gt;, offering vistas of the Lújar and Contraviesa mountain ranges. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986567905/la-cebadilla-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cebadilla Trail&lt;/a&gt;, lead to the historic ghost village of La Cebadilla.

Q: Are the running routes in Capileira suitable for families?
A: While many routes in Capileira involve significant elevation changes and can be challenging, there are easier and moderate options that might be suitable for families with older children or those accustomed to outdoor activities. Look for routes classified as 'easy' or 'moderate' for a more family-friendly experience, often found winding through the lower valleys and connecting the white villages.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails around Capileira?
A: Many trails in the Sierra Nevada Natural Park, which surrounds Capileira, are generally dog-friendly, especially if your dog is well-behaved and kept on a leash. It's always advisable to check specific trail regulations, particularly within the Sierra Nevada National Park, and ensure your dog is prepared for varied terrain and potential encounters with wildlife or livestock. Remember to carry water for both yourself and your pet.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Capileira?
A: The best time for jogging in Capileira is typically spring (April-June) and autumn (September-November). During these seasons, the weather is milder, and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can be very hot, especially at lower altitudes, so early morning or late evening runs are recommended. Winter offers a unique experience with snow-capped peaks, but some higher-altitude trails may be inaccessible or require specialized gear.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Capileira?
A: The running routes in Capileira are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 10 reviews. Over 700 runners have used komoot to explore the varied terrain. Reviewers often praise the dramatic mountain landscapes, the charm of the white villages, and the refreshing mountain water sources found along many trails.

Q: Are there any facilities or places to refill water along the routes?
A: Yes, many trails in the Capileira area feature natural water fountains where runners can refill their bottles with fresh, ice-cold mountain water. This is a welcome feature, especially on longer or more challenging runs. It's still recommended to carry sufficient water, especially on less-trafficked paths or during warmer months.

Q: Where can I park when going for a run in Capileira?
A: Capileira, being a small village, has limited parking directly in the center. However, there are usually designated parking areas at the entrances to the village or slightly outside the main core. Many trailheads are accessible from the village itself, allowing you to park and start your run directly. It's best to arrive early, especially during peak season, to secure a spot.
tips: Chapel of the small town of Las Cebadillas, associated with the construction of the Poqueira hydroelectric plant.

The Poqueira River hydroelectric plant is located at the headwaters of the river from which it takes its name, at the confluence of the latter with the Toril and Nante rivers, 3 kilometers north of the urban center of Capileira (Granada). This power station is the main building of a hydroelectric complex made up of three elements: the power station, the loading chamber and the water conduction channel.
Good point to observe the valley formed by the Poqueira river from the slopes of Loma Ramón.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the La Cebadilla Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts from the village of Capileira. You can find parking within the village, which serves as the main access point for this route and other trails in the Poqueira Valley.

faqs.question: Is the La Cebadilla Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed on the La Cebadilla Trail. However, as this trail is located within the Sierra Nevada National Park, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all trail users.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are ideal for jogging the La Cebadilla Trail. The temperatures are milder, and the scenery is vibrant. In spring, you might even spot Golden Orioles. Summer can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon runs preferable, while winter can bring snow and colder conditions, requiring appropriate gear.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to jog the La Cebadilla Trail itself. It is located within the Sierra Nevada National Park, which is generally open access for hiking and jogging, though certain activities or protected areas might have specific regulations.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the La Cebadilla Trail, and how challenging is it for jogging?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, ranging from paths through olive groves to more rugged mountain sections. It's considered a difficult route for jogging due to some steep and rocky uphill climbs, particularly at the start and after crossing the Poqueira River. It requires noticeable physical effort, making it more suitable for experienced joggers.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog the La Cebadilla Trail?
faqs.answer: The La Cebadilla Trail is approximately 8.6 km (5.3 miles) long. While a walk might take 2.5 to 3 hours, a jogger can expect to complete it in a shorter duration, depending on their pace and fitness level. The total duration for jogging is around 1 hour and 12 minutes.

faqs.question: What interesting sights will I see along the La Cebadilla Trail?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several unique features, including the 'ghost village' of La Cebadilla, a semi-ruined settlement built for hydroelectric workers. The trail also passes the Chapel of La Cebadilla and the Poqueira Hydroelectric Power Plant. You'll also cross the Poqueira River twice and pass through olive groves.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and wildlife can I expect?
faqs.answer: The trail offers breathtaking panoramic views of Mulhacén and Veleta, Spain's highest peaks, and the dramatic Poqueira Gorge. You'll experience a mix of Mediterranean vegetation and rugged mountain landscapes. Keep an eye out for Golden Orioles in spring, and enjoy the diverse flora and fauna of the Sierra Nevada.

faqs.question: Does the La Cebadilla Trail connect with other routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the La Cebadilla Trail intersects with several other paths. Sections of the route overlap with the Acequias del Poqueira, Ruta del Barranco de Poqueira, and Pueblos del Poqueira trails, offering opportunities to explore further if you wish.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trailhead is located in Capileira, one of the picturesque white villages of the Alpujarras. Capileira offers various cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options, making it a convenient base before or after your jog.

faqs.question: What should I wear and bring for jogging this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the varied and sometimes challenging terrain, wear appropriate trail running shoes with good grip. Layers of clothing are recommended, as mountain weather can change quickly. Bring plenty of water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and a map or GPS device, as some sections can be remote.

faqs.question: Is the La Cebadilla Trail suitable for families?
faqs.answer: Due to its difficult rating, including steep and rocky sections, the La Cebadilla Trail is generally not recommended for young children or families seeking an easy stroll. It's better suited for families with older, experienced hikers or joggers who are comfortable with challenging terrain.
